metadata.head_at_handoff_after_this_commit: (this commit -- one parent ahead of 17086a8; the v2 handoff XML is the only file in the diff)
metadata.note: This file SUPPLEMENTS docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE.xml (commit 17086a8). Both v1 and v2 stay on disk for audit history. v2 records the proactive handoff issued by Lead Architect after item 2.3 completed but before context exhaustion would have forced an emergency stop.

ground_truth_at_handoff.git_status_porcelain: M pyproject.toml M tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml M tools/autonomy_budget/config_loader.py M tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_config_loader.py ?? tools/_project_context.py ?? tools/_vikunja_client.py ?? tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ ?? tools/tests/
ground_truth_at_handoff.git_log_oneline_3: 17086a8 (HEAD -> chore/platform-extraction) docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff -- items 2.1+2.2 complete (uncommitted), resume at 2.3 ef0e30e docs(platform_separation): Stage 1→2 handoffs (Guide instance 2→3 + EA Stage 1→Stage 2) aa09f6a chore(platform_separation): between-stages cleanup -- Stage 2 XML defect fixes + REST fallback codification + STATUS A6/A7/A8/A9/A10
ground_truth_at_handoff.stash_zero_untouched: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- DO NOT TOUCH

files_created.file: Verbatim from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml. Exposes ProjectScopeError(ValueError), _require_project_id(value)->int (rejects None, then bool BEFORE int per cleanup-C3, then non-int), and VikunjaClient(base_url, token) with list_tasks(*, project_id, **filters), search_tasks(*, project_id, query, **filters), get_task(task_id). HTTP delegation intentionally stubbed; wired in item 2.6.

Verbatim from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml. Exports DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT = Path(r"C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI"), REGISTRY_RELATIVE = Path(".platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml"), @dataclass(frozen=True) ProjectContext(root, vikunja_project_id, name), _load_registry, _walk_up_for, resolve(cli_root=None, cli_project_id=None). 5-step resolution: cli_root → BLARAI_PROJECT_ROOT env → walk-up registry → walk-up .git → DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT (warns). name_key = f"{root.name.lower()}_project_id" (cleanup-C1 generalized) with generic "project_id" fallback. Rejects None / non-int / bool project_id with ValueError.

pyproject_change.v8_defaults_preservation: Additive only. All previously-discovered paths remain in their original order.

item.name: Per-project allowlist overlay seam
item.design_summary: Removed the BlarAI-specific allowlist entry from tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml (merge_policy.allowlist_paths). Replaced with a comment block pointing at the per-project overlay mechanism. New file tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml holds the BlarAI entry. config_loader.load_config() gains a keyword-only project_overlay_path parameter; when omitted (the production zero-arg call pattern used by all 9 production callers), the loader auto-resolves the active ProjectContext via tools._project_context.resolve(), looks up tools/autonomy_budget/projects/<name>.yaml, and merges any merge_policy.allowlist_paths entries additively (de-duplicated, order-preserving). Defaults-preservation contract verified empirically by the V8 test test_load_config_default_overlay_restores_blarai_path — when called with no args from the BlarAI cwd, the resulting allowlist contains "C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/" exactly as before Stage 2.3.

files_created.file: Per-project overlay holding the BlarAI repo root allowlist entry. Loaded automatically by config_loader.load_config() when ProjectContext.name == "BlarAI". This new directory tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ is also created as part of this item.

files_modified.file: Removed the 5-line BlarAI entry from merge_policy.allowlist_paths (the path "C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/" plus its 4-line preamble comment). Replaced with a 9-line comment block (lines 236-244 in the modified file) explaining that project-specific allowlist entries live in tools/autonomy_budget/projects/<project_name>.yaml and are merged in by config_loader at load time. All 6 host-level allowlist entries (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, VS Code, Git, GitHub Desktop, etc.) remain in config.yaml unchanged because they are NOT project-scoped.

1. Added module constant: PROJECTS_OVERLAY_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "projects". 2. Extended load_config signature: load_config(path: Path | str | None = None, *, project_overlay_path: Path | str | None = None) -> dict. The new parameter is keyword-only and additive (zero-arg callers unaffected). When None, the loader auto-resolves via _resolve_project_overlay_path(); when set to a non-existent path, the overlay is silently skipped (preserves test fixtures). 3. New helper _resolve_project_overlay_path() -> Path | None. Lazy-imports tools._project_context.resolve, calls it with no args, returns PROJECTS_OVERLAY_DIR / f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml". Returns None on any exception (best-effort, never raises). 4. New helper _apply_project_overlay(data, overlay_path). When the overlay file exists, parses it, validates root is a mapping, extracts merge_policy.allowlist_paths, and unions them into data["merge_policy"]["allowlist_paths"] (de-dup, order-preserving). When the file is missing, no-ops silently. When the file is malformed or has wrong shape, raises ConfigLoadError with a "project overlay" prefix. 5. load_config() now invokes _apply_project_overlay(data, project_overlay_path) immediately after _validate_schema(data).
Added 7 new tests under a "Stage 2.3: per-project overlay" section header.

item.v8_defaults_preservation: Verified by test_load_config_default_overlay_restores_blarai_path: load_config() invoked with zero arguments from the BlarAI cwd produces a merge_policy.allowlist_paths list that contains "C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/", identical to pre-2.3 behavior. The full tools/ regression (280 passed, includes the 28 test_merge_policy.py tests that exercise the live config.yaml allowlist) further corroborates this.

uncommitted_on_disk.note: These EIGHT paths stay UNCOMMITTED on disk. Successor folds them into the single Stage 2 commit at item 2.9 along with all 2.4-2.8 work.
uncommitted_on_disk.entry: testpaths additively extended with "tools/tests" as first entry (item 2.1)
BlarAI line removed from merge_policy.allowlist_paths; replaced with a 9-line comment block pointing at the overlay mechanism (item 2.3)
PROJECTS_OVERLAY_DIR constant added; load_config gains keyword-only project_overlay_path; new helpers _resolve_project_overlay_path and _apply_project_overlay (item 2.3)
98 LOC; ProjectContext dataclass + resolve() + registry loader (item 2.2)
contains blarai.yaml (24 LOC, 1278 bytes) -- per-project overlay holding the BlarAI repo root allowlist entry (item 2.3)
contains __init__.py (empty), test_vikunja_client_scope.py (54 LOC, 6 tests), test_project_context.py (100 LOC, 9 tests) (items 2.1+2.2)

work_remaining.item: Add argparse --project-root and --project-id (type=int) to: tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py, weekly_summary.py, dashboards/*.py; tools/gate_stale_cleaner/*.py; tools/autonomy_budget/*.py entrypoints; tools/fleet_ops/*.py wake launchers; tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py; tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/__main__.py. Each entrypoint calls _project_context.resolve(cli_root, cli_project_id) and uses the result wherever a hardcoded BlarAI path appeared.
v2 verification of 2.4 -- every modified entrypoint exposes both flags via --help output.

PowerShell -ProjectRoot param on every .ps1 in tools/scheduled-tasks/ and tools/fleet_ops/. Confirmed touchpoints from XML: tools/scheduled-tasks/wake_launcher.ps1 line 30, tools/fleet_ops/la_merge_approve.ps1 line 38; verify and patch all other .ps1 in those two dirs that hardcode the BlarAI path.
v2 verification of 2.5 -- each .ps1 accepts -ProjectRoot and defaults preserve current behavior for the BlarAI path.

Audit Vikunja call sites -- grep for requests.*localhost:3456, /api/v1/projects/.*/tasks, /api/v1/tasks/all. Replace each direct HTTP call with VikunjaClient. Any list/search call site that cannot supply project_id is a BUG and must be fixed (not deferred).
pytest tools/ --tb=short -q -- must pass with no regressions (current baseline: 280 passed).
v2 verification -- V6 assertions from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml.

Defaults-preservation smoke: $BLARAI_PID = (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.platform\vikunja_project_ids.yaml | Select-String 'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)').Matches.Groups[1].Value; python -m tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest --project-root C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI --project-id $BLARAI_PID. Currently $BLARAI_PID = 3.

Single Stage 2 commit. Per Guide instance 3 acknowledgment #1: ONE commit covering items 2.1-2.8. Use the commit message prescribed in EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml item 2.9. NOTE: pyproject.toml MUST be included in the commit (test discovery for tools/tests depends on it); the XML's literal "git add tools/" instruction is insufficient -- successor uses git add -A or git add tools/ pyproject.toml.
Append-only update to docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md (Stage 2 COMPLETE marker + commit hash + files-touched count). DO NOT modify any other STATUS.md region.

midflight_observations.obs: Predecessor saw a benign "yaml type stubs not installed" lint warning in tools/_project_context.py. PyYAML is a runtime dep; the warning is type-checker only. No action needed.

pyproject.toml change is non-negotiable for item 2.7's pytest discovery -- without "tools/tests" in testpaths, the new tests are not collected. Confirm it survives any rebase / cleanup before item 2.9.
cleanup-C1 (generalized name_key) and cleanup-C3 (bool-before-int guard) from the Stage 2 XML are already implemented in the on-disk files. Successor does NOT need to re-apply them.
VikunjaClient HTTP methods are intentionally stubbed; the wiring lives in item 2.6 audit-and-replace pass. Stub must remain importable until then.
BLARAI_PID resolves to 3 from .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml; this is asserted by test_default_blarai_resolves_to_project_id_3.

load_config has 9 caller files / 11 invocations across tools/ (plus 1 test caller). Predecessor said "8 callers" -- minor count error. Real callers: tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py, tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py, daily_digest.py (×2), dashboard_maintainer.py, weekly_summary.py (×2), welcome_back_digest.py, _vikunja_client.py, tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py, run_live.py. ALL invoke load_config() with zero arguments -- the project_overlay_path parameter added in item 2.3 is keyword-only and defaults to None, so every existing caller continues to receive identical behavior (auto-resolved overlay restores the BlarAI allowlist entry).

tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py exists PRE-Stage-2 (a legacy fleet helper, NOT the new tools/_vikunja_client.py chokepoint from item 2.1). At item 2.6 HTTP audit, treat this file as in-scope: either route it through the new chokepoint or fold it in entirely. Surface the decision in the closing handoff. Naming-collision watchpoint: the two files have identical basenames at different paths; do not import-confuse.

PROJECTS_OVERLAY_DIR is computed from config_loader.py's __file__ location (REPO_ROOT / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "projects"). Post-Stage-4-cutover, this becomes devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/projects/, which is by design (overlay travels with the platform, consistent with the devplatform/projects/ pattern from Stage 1). No code change needed at cutover; the path resolves relative to wherever config_loader.py lives.

_apply_project_overlay's "missing-overlay-is-silent-noop" semantics intentionally preserve existing test_config_loader.py fixtures, which use a minimal GOOD_YAML body that contains no merge_policy block. Without this no-op semantics, every existing test would have to be updated to materialize a merge_policy section. Successor must preserve this when extending the overlay to other config keys (i.e., overlays are additive, never required).

Item 2.3's overlay merge logic is currently scoped to merge_policy.allowlist_paths only. If item 2.4 or later items surface a need to project-scope additional config keys (e.g., merge_policy.blocklist_paths or runaway_thresholds.*), extend _apply_project_overlay() in the same additive style -- DO NOT introduce a generic deep-merge that could clobber config.yaml-side defaults. Per-key explicit merge logic is the design.

The new tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ directory currently contains only blarai.yaml. There is no projects/__init__.py and none should be added -- this directory is a YAML data root, not a Python package.

The 7 new overlay tests live in tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_config_loader.py (NOT in tools/tests/). This is intentional: test_config_loader.py is the existing config_loader test module, and the new tests extend it in-place. Items 2.7 and 2.7.v2 will collect them via the existing tools/autonomy_budget/tests path that pytest already discovers.

Test baseline at end of item 2.3: pytest tools/ --tb=short -q reports 280 passed in 2.41s. Item 2.7's regression bar is "≥ 280 passed, 0 failed". Any drop below 280 is a regression that must be fixed before item 2.9.

critical_carryovers.note: The 9 verbatim acknowledgments from the predecessor's APPROVED comprehension gate remain binding for the successor. Restated:
critical_carryovers.ack: Stage 2 produces exactly ONE commit (item 2.9). All intermediate work stays uncommitted.
Fleet stays paused throughout Stage 2. NEVER call state.resume_fleet(). Fleet remains paused through Stage 4 cutover.
No merge to main during Stage 2. Branch chore/platform-extraction is the only working surface.
stash@{0} (wake_launcher auto-stash on main) is NOT to be popped or applied.
No edits to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc during Stage 2.
All Vikunja access flows through tools/_vikunja_client.py. list_tasks / search_tasks MUST receive int project_id. bool is rejected before int (cleanup-C3).
_project_context.resolve() is the single source for ProjectContext in every refactored entrypoint. Hardcoded C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI paths in entrypoints are a BUG.
V8 defaults preservation: pre-existing pytest discovery, fleet behavior on the BlarAI path, and CLI default behavior all unchanged when --project-root is omitted.
STATUS.md updates at item 2.10 are APPEND-ONLY. Do not edit prior Stage entries.

plus_guide_3.g3: V1-V7 + V8 verification block before closing handoff.

plus_iteration_1_acks.note: Four acknowledgments emitted by the predecessor (this v2 author) during iteration-1 of the comprehension-gate review with Guide instance 3. Successor inherits them.
plus_iteration_1_acks.i1: V0a-V0g pickup verification block must be re-executed (and outputs reported) at the start of every successor session before substantive work begins.
All 10 acknowledgments above (9 from predecessor + 3 from Guide instance 3, deduped) are binding through Stage 4 cutover.
V1-V7 closure verification (per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml) plus per-item V8 (defaults-preservation) must be executed and outputs included in the final closing handoff at Stage 2 completion.

If a successor approaches context exhaustion before item 2.9, it MUST emit a v3 (or higher) mid-stage handoff XML following the same schema as v1/v2, commit ONLY that file (docs-only commit), and stop. NO partial-commit refactor work. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.

ea_handoff_stage2_midstage_v2.single_commit_discipline: Items 2.1, 2.2, AND 2.3 work stays UNCOMMITTED on disk. The successor runs git add -A (or explicit git add tools/ pyproject.toml) and produces the SINGLE Stage 2 commit at item 2.9 using the commit message prescribed in EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml item 2.9. The new tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ directory and its blarai.yaml content are part of that single commit.

successor_pickup_verification.command: git log --oneline -1 # expect: this v2 handoff commit (parent: 17086a8)

git status --porcelain # expect EXACTLY 8 entries: M pyproject.toml M tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml M tools/autonomy_budget/config_loader.py M tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_config_loader.py ?? tools/_project_context.py ?? tools/_vikunja_client.py ?? tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ ?? tools/tests/

Test-Path tools/_vikunja_client.py, tools/_project_context.py, tools/tests/__init__.py, tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py, tools/tests/test_project_context.py, tools/autonomy_budget/projects/, tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml # expect 7x True
Get-Content tools/autonomy_budget/state.json | Select-String fleet_paused # expect: "fleet_paused": true
git stash list # expect: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 (DO NOT POP)

risk_label_hygiene_carryforward.note: The iteration-1 review by Guide instance 3 flagged this v2-author's "R5"/"R6" risk labels as colliding with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md §0.6 R5/R6 (which are RESOLVED in cleanup A6). Successor MUST use distinct, descriptive labels for any new risk concerns rather than recycling numeric R-prefixed identifiers. Recommended labels for likely Stage 2.4-2.8 concerns:
risk_label_hygiene_carryforward.label: Use when adding --project-root / --project-id to entrypoints that may already define conflicting flags.
Use when _project_context.resolve() may emit the "fell back to DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT" warning under unexpected cwd conditions.
Use for the tools/_vikunja_client.py vs tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py basename collision (per midflight obs v2-2).
Use when extending _apply_project_overlay to additional config keys (per midflight obs v2-5).
risk_label_hygiene_carryforward.classification: INFORMATIONAL CARRY-FORWARD ONLY. No re-emission of comprehension gate required for this point.

do_not.prohibition: Do NOT merge chore/platform-extraction to main during Stage 2.
Do NOT git stash pop / git stash apply on stash@{0}. It is a stale wake_launcher auto-stash.
Do NOT edit CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc.
Do NOT call state.resume_fleet(...). Fleet stays paused through Stage 4 cutover.
Do NOT produce more than one Stage 2 commit (item 2.9). All other artifacts (handoff XMLs, etc.) are out of Stage 2 scope.
Do NOT touch any file under docs/platform_separation/ other than (a) STATUS.md append-only at item 2.10, and (b) any handoff XML this successor itself emits to ea_outputs/.
Do NOT delete or "clean up" the v1 mid-stage handoff (EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE.xml, commit 17086a8) or this v2 handoff. Both stay on disk for audit history.
Do NOT modify the predecessor's content in v1 -- v2 is additive history, not a rewrite.
Do NOT add tools/autonomy_budget/projects/__init__.py. The projects/ dir is a YAML data root, not a Python package.
